stories.title: Fail-Closed Completion Gate (Stop hook + OPA/Conftest + GitHub ruleset)
acceptance_criteria: evaluate_stop filters to in_scope_items and counts ONLY in-scope items in every gate (Req 5.7).

HANDOFF triggers (cap phase-discriminated on spec_pass_count>=7 / iteration_count>=25, then budget token_cost_usd>=TOKEN_BUDGET, then implementation-phase check_no_progress N=3) each return allow()/exit 0 with a HANDOFF summary BEFORE the unproven-items gate.
The completion gate computes not_proven = [i for i in in_scope_items if i.status != 'proven'] and blocks (exit 2) when non-empty; a failed in-scope item blocks exactly like unproven.
Empty-coverage gate blocks (exit 2) when in_scope_items == []; spec-completion gate blocks while violation_count > 0 and fails closed when violation_count < 0.
with_reentrancy_guard sets stop_hook_active before evaluation, releases only on allow, short-circuits to allow() if already set; unhandled exception exits 2 (fail closed).

coverage_query.rego denies merge on: any in-scope item status != 'proven'; any in-scope proven item failing complete_evidence (all four fields != ''); zero in-scope items (INIT). coverage-gate.yml treats ANY non-zero Conftest exit as a merge BLOCK.
No gate reads a prediction (CHECK-5); Postgres-unavailable falls back to file-backed state reaching the SAME conservative/blocking decision (Note N-25), never fail-open.
Properties 4, 5, 22 pass under --hypothesis-seed=0; CHECK-1/CHECK-3/CHECK-5 pass in formal_verification_merged.py.
Phase-0 OPA stub (status-only) is weaker than the Phase-1 full policy; the coverage-gate required-check registration survives the stub->full swap (re-register).
tasks: stop_hook.py evaluate_stop completion gate + reentrancy guard
Completion-gate property/Z3 verification + Plane gate mirroring

stories.title: Supply-Chain & Security Gates (SAST + secrets + SLSA + sandbox)
acceptance_criteria: The CI SAST gate (codeql.yml + Semgrep workflow) fails the run on any HIGH/CRITICAL finding; PostToolUse surfaces Semgrep findings as next-turn feedback only (exit 1, never blocks) and does NOT enforce the threshold.

secrets-scan.yml: on pull_request + protected-branch pushes; gitleaks (pinned) on the PR diff; SARIF report; .gitleaks.toml ruleset + false-positive allowlist; fails on any secret. Fixture test: planted AWS key fails, clean diff passes (Property 32).
The PreToolUse secret-block guard scans args/prompt/span-attrs/URL against secret regexes and blocks carrying calls with exit 2 (Property 20) — a live prevention gate.

The sandbox confines writes to the per-slice worktree mounted in, denies network egress by default (explicit audited allowlist exceptions), and is the named enforcer of the Implementer's 'worktree only' permission.
SLSA provenance is generated on merge and verifiable via gh attestation verify; the SLSA release workflow jointly satisfies REQ-AUDIT-003 with the audit-chain verifier.
secrets-scan is a REQUIRED merge gate (Phase 1) registered in the GitHub ruleset and documented in docs/github-ruleset.md (canonical list = task 14.2).
Build-order split: the secrets-scan.yml workflow may be created early (wave 33) but merge-blocking enforcement waits until task 45's gitleaks scan logic lands.

stories.title: DeepEval Eval-Gating in CI (faithfulness + answer relevancy)
acceptance_criteria: tools/deepeval_gate.py calls assert_test() on faithfulness >= 0.8 and answer relevancy >= 0.7, reading thresholds from the task-44 execution-bounds/threshold registry (operator-overridable), not hard-coded as sole source.
The judge is pinned to a named deterministic model+provider: DEFAULT claude-opus-4-8 via Anthropic on Vertex AI, temperature=0, fixed seed, pinned snapshot — recorded in the gate config / workflow.
The golden-set dataset lives under tests/eval/, is version-controlled (reproducible), and is seeded from Verifier-run evidence.

Invariant reconciliation is explicit: deepeval_gate does NOT self-grade the agent's own output and is NOT a probabilistic completeness prediction — it is a deterministic acceptance check on a fixed dataset scoped to product-output quality, independent of the completion gates.
An empty-evaluation CI run passes vacuously (green, zero cases); fail-closed on judge-LLM failure after bounded retry (<= 2).
deepeval-gate is a REQUIRED status check (Phase-4 task 40.6) recorded via GitHub check-run history + SLSA provenance (NOT the hook hash chain — REQ-27.8 scope rule).
A smoke/integration test asserts the build fails when a metric is below threshold (no Z3 oracle; this is the named verification mechanism).

stories.title: OWASP ZAP DAST Baseline Security Scan
acceptance_criteria: .github/workflows/zap-baseline.yml uses zaproxy/action-baseline@v0.12.0 with fail_action: true, triggered on pull_request (optionally a protected-branch target filter).
A CI job step boots the app/service at a known http://localhost:<port> and the action target input is populated with that URL (v1 baseline target = unauthenticated localhost).
The scan is baseline/passive only — no active probing (zero false-positive risk from active probes).
zap-baseline is registered as a REQUIRED CI status check (Phase-4 task 40.5) in the GitHub ruleset, documented in docs/github-ruleset.md (canonical list = task 14.2).

The gate decision is recorded via GitHub check-run history + SLSA provenance (NOT the hook hash chain — REQ-27.8); the absence of a Z3 Property/CHECK is documented intent, consistent with secrets-scan/coverage-gate/deepeval-gate.
tasks: zap-baseline.yml DAST workflow + CI app stand-up

stories.title: OpenFeature/flagd Agent Code Kill-Switch
acceptance_criteria: The flagd/ directory holds a flag-definitions file (flagd.json), provider config, and a self-hosted deployment manifest; the flag schema enumerates kill.initializer/kill.implementer/kill.verifier/kill.research/kill.all (boolean, one per gated capability).

Each agent entry point queries the OpenFeature client at start-of-turn; a set kill.<capability> (or kill.all) makes the capability HARD-REFUSE (refuse the gated tool call / skip the capability — analogous to a PreToolUse exit-2 block).
ONE propagation mechanism is chosen and the <=30 s guarantee is tied to it: DEFAULT flagd gRPC streaming; documented fallback file-based provider with explicit poll interval <= 30 s.
Unreachable flagd -> fail-closed (capability DISABLED, flag treated as true); the kill-switch never fails open.
Flag toggles are restricted to the Delivery Owner / Operator role; each toggle is authenticated and recorded.
Every toggle (and every start-of-turn kill-refusal) appends a gate_audit_log entry via audit_log.append(...) AND emits an OTel gate-decision event to the shared trace endpoint.

A <=30 s propagation test verifies the chosen provider mechanism disables the capability without a process restart; the unreachable-flagd fail-closed behavior is tested (runtime/CI-verified, no Z3 oracle).
The OpenFeature kill-switch invariant (set/unreachable flag implies capability refused, fail-closed) is documented as a runtime/CI-verified invariant.
tasks: OpenFeature SDK wiring into agent entry points
